headlines.title: Supreme Court allows states to count mail-in ballots that arrive late, rejecting RNC challenge
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — Rejecting a Republican National Committee challenge, the Supreme Court ruled Monday that elections officials may count mail-in ballots that arrive after Election Day if they were postmarked beforehand

headlines.title: Justices rule Trump cannot fire Fed member, but grant more power over independent agencies
headlines.description: The Supreme Court delivered a setback to President Donald Trump, rejecting his attempt to fire Federal Reserve board member Lisa Cook, while in a separate case giving him a freer hand to exert control over other hitherto independent federal agencies.

headlines.title: Supreme Court rules that broad cell phone location data sweeps require warrants
headlines.description: In a ruling applying individual constitutional protections to new technology, the Supreme Court on Monday ruled that sweeping use of cell phone location data requires a warrant.

headlines.title: Confederate flag image removed from North Carolina booth at Great American State Fair
headlines.description: Images of the Confederate flag were removed from the North Carolina state booth at the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C., in yet another controversy to blight the events meant to mark the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence

headlines.title: Senate Ethics panel dismisses misconduct complaint against Ruben Gallego
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — The Senate Ethics Committee has dismissed allegations of misconduct by Sen.

headlines.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump's bid to overturn E. Jean Carroll verdict
headlines.description: The Supreme Court handed a loss to President Donald Trump by refusing to hear his attempt to overturn a jury's finding that he defamed writer E. Jean Carroll. NBC News' Laura Jarrett has the latest on E. Jean Carroll winning her case.

headlines.title: Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed member Lisa Cook
headlines.description: The Supreme Court is rejecting President Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve board member Lisa Cook, but did grant him more power over other independent federal agencies in a separate decision. NBC News' Laura Jarrett reports on the two rulings and how the justices differed in their views of the president's authority.

headlines.title: Trump Taps Oklahoma State Trooper as Next ICE Director
headlines.description: President Donald Trump announced he is nominating former Oklahoma State Trooper Lance Schroyer to lead ICE as its next director. Schroyer is a close ally of Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin. It comes as Trump spent the weekend touring sites of his current and future renovations of the nation’s capital. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Supreme Court to weigh Arizona’s proof of citizenship voting law
headlines.description: The Supreme Court will consider whether Arizona rules requiring voters to show proof of citizenship when registering to vote violate federal law.

headlines.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s push to overturn $5 million E. Jean Carroll verdict
headlines.description: The Supreme Court refused to hear President Donald Trump’s attempt to overturn a jury's finding that he sexually assaulted and defamed writer E. Jean Carroll.

headlines.title: U.S. and Iran set for new talks, Trump says, after Hormuz clashes threatened peace deal
headlines.description: JERUSALEM — The United States and Iran will hold new talk Tuesday in Qatar, President Donald Trump said Monday, after the two sides appeared to step back from a wave of attacks that threatened to derail peace efforts.“IRAN HAS REQUESTED A MEETING.
